[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d7/Shilin_Guangji_Phagspa_Hundred_Family_Surnames.jpg/250px-Shilin_Guangji_Phagspa_Hundred_Family_Surnames.jpg)](/wiki/File:Shilin_Guangji_Phagspa_Hundred_Family_Surnames.jpg)

*Hundred Family Surnames* written in [Chinese characters](/wiki/Chinese_characters "Chinese characters") and [Phagspa script](/wiki/Phagspa_script "Phagspa script"), from *[Shilin Guangji](/wiki/Shilin_Guangji "Shilin Guangji")* written by [Chen Yuanjing](/wiki/Chen_Yuanjing "Chen Yuanjing") in the [Yuan dynasty](/wiki/Yuan_dynasty "Yuan dynasty")

The ***Hundred Family Surnames*** ([Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"): 百家姓), commonly known as ***Bai Jia Xing***,[[1]](#cite_note-Lee2011-1) also translated as ***Hundreds of Chinese Surnames***,[[2]](#cite_note-HanksCoates2016-2) is a classic [Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language") text composed of common [Chinese surnames](/wiki/Chinese_surname "Chinese surname"). An unknown author compiled the book during the [Song dynasty](/wiki/Song_dynasty "Song dynasty") (960–1279).[[3]](#cite_note-KS-3) The book lists 504 surnames. Of these, 444 are single-character surnames and 60 are [double-character surnames](/wiki/Chinese_compound_surname "Chinese compound surname"). About 800 names have been derived from the original ones.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In the dynasties following the Song, the 13th-century *[Three Character Classic](/wiki/Three_Character_Classic "Three Character Classic")*, the *Hundred Family Surnames*, and the 6th-century *[Thousand Character Classic](/wiki/Thousand_Character_Classic "Thousand Character Classic")* came to be known as *San Bai Qian* (Three, Hundred, Thousand), from the first character in their titles. They served as instructional books for children,[[5]](#cite_note-5) becoming the almost universal introductory literary texts for students (almost exclusively boys) from elite backgrounds and even for a number of ordinary villagers. Each text was available in many versions, printed cheaply and available to all since they did not become superseded. When a student had memorized all three, he had a knowledge of roughly 2,000 [characters](/wiki/Chinese_characters "Chinese characters"). Since Chinese did not use an alphabet, this was an effective, though time-consuming, way of studying character-recognition before going on to understanding texts and writing characters.[[6]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTERawski197946–48-6)

## Form

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hundred_Family_Surnames&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Form")]

The work is a rhyming poem in lines of eight characters. The surnames are not listed in order of commonality. According to Song dynasty scholar Wang Mingqing (王明清), the first four surnames listed represent the most important families in the empire at the time, particularly in the [Jiangnan Region](/wiki/Jiangnan_Region "Jiangnan Region"):[[7]](#cite_note-7)

* 1st: [Zhao](/wiki/Zhao_(surname) "Zhao (surname)") (趙) is the surname of the [Song dynasty](/wiki/Song_dynasty "Song dynasty") emperors.
* 2nd: [Qian](/wiki/Qian_(surname) "Qian (surname)") (錢) is the surname of the kings of [Wuyue](/wiki/Wuyue "Wuyue").
* 3rd: [Sun](/wiki/Sun_(surname) "Sun (surname)") (孫) is the surname of the queen [Sun Taizhen](/wiki/Sun_Taizhen "Sun Taizhen") of Wuyue king [Qian Chu](/wiki/Qian_Chu "Qian Chu").
* 4th: [Li](/wiki/Li_(surname_%E6%9D%8E) "Li (surname 李)") (李) is the surname of the kings of [Southern Tang](/wiki/Southern_Tang "Southern Tang").

The next four, [Zhou 周](/wiki/Zhou_(surname) "Zhou (surname)"), [Wu 吳](/wiki/Wu_(surname) "Wu (surname)"), [Zheng 鄭](/wiki/Zheng_(surname) "Zheng (surname)"), and [Wang 王](/wiki/Wang_(surname) "Wang (surname)"), were the surnames of the other wives of [Qian Chu](/wiki/Qian_Chu "Qian Chu"), the last king of Wuyue.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Prevalence in modern times

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hundred_Family_Surnames&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Prevalence in modern times")]

In 2013 the [Fuxi Institution](https://www.fuxiinstitution.org.cn/?lang=en-US) compiled a ranking of the 400 most common surnames in China.[[9]](#cite_note-:0-10)

### Under 300th most common

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hundred_Family_Surnames&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Under 300th most common")]

According to the study, the following surnames from the *Hundred Family Surnames* are not among the 300 most common surnames:[[9]](#cite_note-:0-10)

* Yōng 雍 – 339th
* Píng 平 – 315th
* Mǐ 米 – 316th
* Zhàn 湛 – 369th
